original_hypothesis.title: MagicLP setParameters Allows Implementation Owner to Drain All Pool Reserves
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: setParameters allows the implementation owner to specify arbitrary baseOutAmount and quoteOutAmount, which are transferred to an arbitrary assetTo address before resetting reserves. Combined with the minBaseReserve/minQuoteReserve check being against pre-transfer reserves, the owner can drain the entire pool's base and quote token balances in a single transaction, stealing all LP funds.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Line 422-434: `_transferBaseOut(assetTo, baseOutAmount)` and `_transferQuoteOut(assetTo, quoteOutAmount)` transfer arbitrary amounts. Lines 416-418 check min reserves against pre-transfer _BASE_RESERVE_/_QUOTE_RESERVE_, not post-transfer balances.
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: MIMSwap.s.sol shows ownership transferred to safe.ops multisig, which provides some protection

proof_of_signal.pattern: onlyImplementationOwner can set arbitrary baseOutAmount/quoteOutAmount transferred to arbitrary assetTo; minBaseReserve check at 416 is against pre-transfer reserves, not post-transfer

preconditions: Attacker has implementation owner privileges (key compromise or malicious admin)

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: setParameters combines parameter changes with arbitrary token extraction in single transaction
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Any time admin key is available

operational_questions: Is the implementation owner a multisig with adequate threshold?
Are there monitoring alerts for setParameters calls?
Does the minReserve check provide meaningful protection?
validation_requirements: Verify setParameters with baseOutAmount = full reserve succeeds

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Call setParameters with baseOutAmount=full base balance, quoteOutAmount=full quote balance, minBaseReserve=0, minQuoteReserve=0
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: All pool tokens transferred to attacker's assetTo address
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Pool is permanently bricked with zero reserves

potential_operational_impact.administrative_compromise: Complete loss of all pool assets across all clones sharing same implementation
potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: All pools controlled by same implementation owner vulnerable
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: All pool operations for affected pools

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
